stem: A agriculture study measures crop yield across 5 regions, each containing multiple farms. region A has 20 observations with mean 83.1 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 7.4). region B has 20 observations with mean 92.6 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 5.5). region C has 8 observations with mean 77.5 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 5.0). region D has 15 observations with mean 55.3 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 8.7). region E has 10 observations with mean 80.3 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 9.1). The between-region standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 6.4 and 15.9 tonnes/hectare. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true crop yield for region E, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-region variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for region E: 80.2 tonnes/hectare (shrunk from raw 80.3). Range [80.1, 80.3] as between-region SD varies from 6.4 to 15.9. Pooled mean: 79.0.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 79.0 tonnes/hectare (weighted average of 5 region means).
Shrinkage factor for region E: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/10). At σ_between=10.6: B = 0.931.
Interval [80.1, 80.3] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (80.3) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (79.0) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
